LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT HAVING REVIEWED THE PLEADINGS AND HAVING HEARD ORAL ARGUMENT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF DALE PALMER'S AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT: PLAINTIFF DALE PALMER'S MOTION TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. CALIFORNIA'S STRONG POLICY OF LIBERALITY IN AMENDMENT OF PLEADINGS SUPPORTS THE MOTION. (SEE, E.G., HOWARD V. COUNTY OF SAN DIEGO (2010) 184 CAL.APP.4TH 1422, 1428 ["THE POLICY FAVORING AMENDMENT IS SO STRONG THAT IT IS A RARE CASE IN WHICH DENIAL OF LEAVE TO AMEND CAN BE JUSTIFIED."].) DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT PLAINTIFF UNDULY DELAYED IN SEEKING AMENDMENT OR THAT THEY WOULD BE PREJUDICED BY ALLOWING THE AMENDMENT. DEFENDANTS MAY RAISE ANY CHALLENGE TO THE ADEQUACY OF THE AMENDED COMPLAINT BY WAY OF DEMURRER, MOTION TO STRIKE, OR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION. (SEE BROWN V. RALPHS GROCERY CO. (2018) 28 CAL.APP.5TH 824, 843 ["THE 'BETTER COURSE OF ACTION' IS TO ALLOW A PLAINTIFF TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT 'AND THEN LET THE PARTIES TEST ITS LEGAL SUFFICIENCY IN OTHER APPROPRIATE PROCEEDINGS.'"].) ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF DALE PALMER'S MOTION TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR. DUPLICATE ENTRY. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)
PLAINTIFFS OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT CHEETAH GROUPS MOTION TO COMPEL MEDICAL EXAM (TRANSACTION ID # 100075595) FILED BY PLAINTIFF PALMER, DALE
REPLY BRIEF IN SUPPORT OF DEFT. RESTAURANT CHEETAH GROUP, INC'S MOTION TO COMPEL INDEPENDENT MEDICAL EXAMINATION (TRANSACTION ID # 100076230) FILED BY DEFENDANT RESTAURANT CHEETAH GROUP, INC.

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT RESTAURANT CHEETAH GROUP, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL INDEPENDENT MEDICAL EXAMINATION. MOTION IS DENIED FOR DEFENDANT CHEETAH GROUP'S FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH THE MEET AND CONFER REQUIREMENTS OF C.C.P. 2032.250. THE COURT NOTES THAT DEFENDANT CHEETAH GROUP DID NOT MEET AND CONFER PRIOR TO THE I.M.E. AND PROCEEDED WITH THE I.M.E, THAT THE I.M.E. WAS NOT SUSPENDED PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 2032.510, AND THAT DEFENDANT CHEETAH GROUP DID NOT MOVE FOR A PROTECTIVE ORDER PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 2032.510. THEREFORE, UNDER THESE FACTS, DEFENDANT CHEETAH GROUP ALREADY HAD ITS ONE I.M.E. AS PROVIDED BY C.C.P. 2032.220. TO THE EXTENT THAT THE MOTION WAS BROUGHT PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 2032.310, THE MOTION IS DENIED FOR DEFECTIVE NOTICE OF MOTION WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT CHEETAH GROUP'S RIGHT TO BRING ANOTHER PROPERLY NOTICED MOTION PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 2032.310. THE COURT MOREOVER NOTES THAT THE PARTIES' SUBSEQUENT "MEET AND CONFER" DOES NOT APPEAR TO BE A REASONABLE, GOOD FAITH ATTEMPT AT INFORMAL RESOLUTION OF THE ISSUES. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE PRO TEM: WILLIAM LYNN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-19-2019 Vacated

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Independent Medical Examination
Off Calendar Jul-08-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Paul Alaga, Esq. 415-581-0885 for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

Counsel stipulates to the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT RESTAURANT CHEETAH GROUP, INC.'s Motion To Compel Independent Medical Examination.

Motion is denied for Defendant Cheetah Group's failure to comply with the meet and confer requirements of C.C.P. 2032.250. The court notes that Defendant Cheetah Group did not meet and confer prior to the I.M.E. and proceeded with the I.M.E, that the I.M.E. was not suspended pursuant to C.C.P. 2032.510, and that Defendant Cheetah Group did not move for a protective order pursuant to C.C.P. 2032.510. Therefore, under these facts, Defendant Cheetah Group already had its one I.M.E. as provided by C.C.P. 2032.220. To the extent that the motion was brought pursuant to C.C.P. 2032.310, the motion is denied for defective notice of motion without prejudice to Defendant Cheetah Group's right to bring another properly noticed motion pursuant to C.C.P. 2032.310. The court moreover notes that the parties' subsequent "meet and confer" does not appear to be a reasonable, good faith attempt at informal resolution of the issues.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

Amended Notice Of Motion To Amend The Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-20-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Roozbeh Falahati, esq. (415-581-0885) of Bryant Law Group for Plaintiff, Dale Palmer.
Douglas A. Sears, esq. (916-978-3434) of Matheny Sears...LLP for Defendant, Restaurant Cheetah Group, Inc.
Jeffrey R. Marquart, esq. (949-589-0150) for Defendant, Sir Carson, via Court Call.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DALE PALMER's Amended Notice Of Motion To Amend The Complaint:

Plaintiff Dale Palmer's motion to amend the complaint is granted. California's strong policy of liberality in amendment of pleadings supports the motion. (See, e.g., Howard v. County of San Diego (2010) 184 Cal.App.4th 1422, 1428 ["The policy favoring amendment is so strong that it is a rare case in which denial of leave to amend can be justified."].) Defendants have not shown that Plaintiff unduly delayed in seeking amendment or that they would be prejudiced by allowing the amendment. Defendants may raise any challenge to the adequacy of the amended complaint by way of demurrer, motion to strike, or summary judgment/adjudication. (See Brown v. Ralphs Grocery Co. (2018) 28 Cal.App.5th 824, 843 ["The 'better course of action' is to allow a plaintiff to amend the complaint 'and then let the parties test its legal sufficiency in other appropriate proceedings.'"].)

Order is signed in open court.
